event: Russian Orthodox cathedral in a modern city in winter
symbols: onion domes — the flame of prayer ascending to heaven, a distinctive element of Russian Orthodox sacred architecture
gold crosses atop domes — the centrality of Christ's cross in Orthodox faith, shining even under winter skies
cathedral amid apartment blocks — the persistence of sacred space in the modern secular city
snow and winter light — the endurance of faith through cold and hardship, the spiritual winter that cannot extinguish the Church
description: A painting depicting a large Russian Orthodox cathedral with multiple blue-gray onion domes topped by gold crosses, set in a modern urban environment. The cathedral's pale stone walls and ornate architectural details contrast with the drab, boxy apartment buildings visible in the background to the left. The scene is set in winter: snow covers the ground, bare trees line the street, and the sky is a flat, overcast gray. A few pedestrians in winter coats walk along the snow-covered sidewalks and road. Street lamps and power lines punctuate the composition.
